differential_diagnoses: Eosinophilic meningitis of other etiology (e.g., idiopathic or viral)
next_steps: Perform contrast-enhanced MRI of the brain and spine to assess for inflammation, nerve root enhancement, or lesions.
Send CSF for Angiostrongylus cantonensis PCR and antibody testing via reference laboratory or CDC.
Perform serologic testing for A. cantonensis, Gnathostoma, and Toxocara in serum.
Consult infectious disease specialist for management of eosinophilic meningitis and guidance on anti-helminthic therapy versus corticosteroids.
Continue monitoring for signs of intracranial hypertension and worsening encephalopathy.
Test CSF for a broader panel of viral pathogens including enteroviruses and West Nile virus.
